assertion_results.text: Impact map or task descriptions explicitly flag at least 3 ambiguities from the feature description
assertion_results.evidence: The impact-map.md 'Feature Ambiguities Identified' section explicitly lists 5 ambiguities: (1) 'Search should be faster' — no quantitative performance target, (2) 'Results should be more relevant' — no definition of relevance or ranking criteria, (3) 'Add filters' — no specification of which filters or fields, (4) 'Should be fast enough' — no measurable threshold, (5) 'Don't break existing functionality' — standard backward compatibility. This exceeds the minimum of 3.

assertion_results.text: Plan acknowledges that 'Better UI' (non-MVP) cannot be planned without design mockups or frontend repository and excludes it from scope
assertion_results.evidence: The impact-map.md does not mention 'Better UI' at all. The non-MVP requirement 'Better UI — Make it look nicer' from the feature description is silently omitted from the impact map and tasks, but there is no explicit acknowledgment that it cannot be planned without design mockups or frontend repository. None of the output files contain any text referencing 'Better UI', design mockups, or frontend repository exclusion.

assertion_results.text: Tasks still follow the task-description-template.md format despite ambiguous requirements
assertion_results.evidence: All four tasks follow the template structure from task-description-template.md. Each contains: Repository, Target Branch, Description, Files to Modify (and Files to Create where applicable), Implementation Notes, Reuse Candidates, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements, Verification Commands, and Dependencies sections. Optional sections are omitted per the template rules (e.g., Task 2 omits Files to Create since it only modifies). The format matches the prescribed template.

assertion_results.text: Tasks document assumptions where they fill in missing details, labeled as assumptions pending clarification
assertion_results.evidence: While the impact-map.md documents ambiguities and how they were interpreted (e.g., 'Interpreted as: add PostgreSQL full-text search indexes'), the individual task descriptions (task-1 through task-4) do not contain any sections or labels identifying 'assumptions' or 'pending clarification'. The tasks present implementation details as definitive decisions rather than marking them as assumptions that need confirmation. The impact map says 'These interpretations should be confirmed with the product owner before implementation begins' but the tasks themselves do not carry forward any assumption labeling.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All four tasks contain a '## Repository' section with exactly one repository name: Task 1: 'trustify-backend', Task 2: 'trustify-backend', Task 3: 'trustify-backend', Task 4: 'trustify-backend'. No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All four tasks contain: '## Target Branch' (set to 'main'), '## Description' (with substantive content), '## Acceptance Criteria' (with checkbox items), and '## Test Requirements' (with checkbox items). Verified in task-1-fulltext-search-migration.md, task-2-refactor-search-service-fulltext.md, task-3-add-search-filter-parameters.md, and task-4-search-integration-tests.md.

assertion_results.text: File paths in Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths from the repo-backend.md mock repository structure manifest, not invented paths
assertion_results.evidence: File paths in tasks reference paths from repo-backend.md: Task 1 modifies migration/src/lib.rs, entity/src/sbom.rs, entity/src/advisory.rs, entity/src/package.rs (all in manifest) and creates migration/src/m0002_fulltext_search/mod.rs (follows the pattern of migration/src/m0001_initial/mod.rs from manifest). Task 2 modifies modules/search/src/service/mod.rs and modules/search/src/lib.rs (both in manifest). Task 3 modifies modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs, modules/search/src/service/mod.rs, common/src/db/query.rs (all in manifest). Task 4 modifies tests/api/search.rs (in manifest). All paths trace back to the repo-backend.md directory tree.

assertion_results.text: Implementation Notes in every task reference specific file paths and code patterns from the repository, not abstract or generic guidance
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1 references migration/src/m0001_initial/mod.rs, entity/src/sbom.rs, entity/src/advisory.rs, entity/src/package.rs with specific patterns (SeaORM migration API, field declaration patterns). Task 2 references modules/search/src/service/mod.rs, common/src/db/query.rs, common/src/model/paginated.rs, common/src/error.rs with specific patterns (PaginatedResults<T>, Result<T, AppError> with .context()). Task 3 references modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/list.rs (Axum Query extractor pattern), modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/summary.rs (severity field), modules/fundamental/src/package/model/summary.rs (license field), common/src/db/query.rs. Task 4 references tests/api/search.rs, tests/api/sbom.rs, tests/api/advisory.rs with specific patterns (assert_eq!(resp.status(), StatusCode::OK)).

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Target Branch section set to 'main'
assertion_results.evidence: All four tasks have '## Target Branch' followed by 'main': Task 1 line 7-8, Task 2 line 7-8, Task 3 line 7-8, Task 4 line 7-8.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' (or sha256-adf). The digest is computed by re-fetching the description from the API and running scripts/sha256-digest.py
assertion_results.evidence: None of the output files contain any SHA-256 digest comments. There is no file or content in the outputs directory that includes '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest:' or any sha256-md:/sha256-adf: markers. The outputs consist only of impact-map.md and four task description files, none of which contain digest information.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: The task descriptions reference docs/constraints.md sections (e.g., 'Per docs/constraints.md §2 (Commit Rules)', 'Per docs/constraints.md §5 (Code Change Rules)') in Implementation Notes, but none include the prescribed applicability rationale format 'Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'. The references are free-form prose without the required structured rationale. No 'Applies:' lines are present in any task's Implementation Notes.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a priority set (not 'Undefined'), every created task's additional_fields includes 'priority' with the inherited priority name. When the Feature's priority is 'Undefined', the priority key is omitted entirely from additional_fields (not set to null or 'Undefined')
assertion_results.evidence: The feature fixture (feature-ambiguous.md) has Priority: Normal, which is not 'Undefined', so every task should include priority in additional_fields. However, none of the four task description files contain an 'additional_fields' section or any mention of inheriting or propagating the priority value 'Normal'. The task descriptions do not contain any priority field configuration.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a non-empty fixVersions array and the fixVersion scope config (from ### Jira Field Defaults in CLAUDE.md) is 'task' or 'both' (or absent, defaulting to 'both'), every created task's additional_fields includes 'fixVersions' with the inherited version(s). When fixVersion scope is 'feature' or the Feature has no fixVersions, the fixVersions key is omitted entirely from additional_fields
assertion_results.evidence: The feature fixture has Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.6.0 (non-empty), and the CLAUDE.md has no ### Jira Field Defaults section (so fixVersion scope defaults to 'both'). Therefore every task should include fixVersions in additional_fields. However, none of the four task description files contain an 'additional_fields' section or any mention of inheriting or propagating fixVersions. The value 'RHTPA 1.6.0' does not appear in any task file.

assertion_results.text: The summary comment on the feature issue (Step 6c) includes the inherited priority and fixVersion values that were propagated to tasks, or states they were omitted and why
assertion_results.evidence: There is no summary comment output file in the outputs directory. The outputs contain only impact-map.md and four task descriptions. No file contains a summary comment referencing Step 6c, and no file discusses priority or fixVersion propagation to tasks.
